summary.para: Cleans up a paragraph by removing proofErr markers and merging adjacent text-only runs that were artificially split by Word's editing infrastructure.

Word splits runs for several non-content reasons:
Spelling/grammar markers (<w:proofErr>) split text around flagged words.
Revision tracking (rsidR attributes) creates new runs for each editing session.
Bookmarks (<w:bookmarkStart/End>) insert markers between runs.
This method only removes proofErr elements (disposable spell-check hints that Word regenerates automatically). Bookmarks are intentionally preserved because
they carry document semantics: cross-references, TOC targets, hyperlink anchors, and form field bindings all depend on them.
Placeholders split by bookmarks are handled by the cross-run fallback without needing to alter the XML structure.
After proofErr removal, adjacent text-only runs with identical formatting are merged. Runs containing structural elements like <w:tab/>
are never merged, preserving intentionally separate runs (e.g. "Amount Billed: $[TOTAL_CHARGES]" where a tab separates the label from the value).

member.name: M:IronWord.APIWrapper.Parse.WordXmlToObjectModel.RemoveProofErrors(DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Wordprocessing.Paragraph)
member.summary:
Removes all ProofError elements from a paragraph. These are disposable spell/grammar check metadata that Word regenerates whenever the document
is opened. Removing them has zero semantic impact on the document, but eliminates a source of run splitting that breaks placeholder replacement
and causes model/XML structural mismatches during save.

member.name: M:IronWord.APIWrapper.Parse.WordXmlToObjectModel.MergeAdjacentTextOnlyRuns(DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Wordprocessing.Paragraph)
member.summary: Merges adjacent Run elements that have identical formatting and contain only text content. Runs with structural elements (tabs, breaks, images, drawings) are never merged, preserving intentional document structure.

member.name: M:IronWord.APIWrapper.Parse.WordXmlToObjectModel.IsTextOnlyRun(DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Wordprocessing.Run)
member.summary: Checks whether a run contains only text elements (and optionally RunProperties). Returns false if the run contains tabs, breaks, images, drawings, or any other non-text structural content that indicates it was intentionally separate.

member.name: M:IronWord.APIWrapper.Parse.WordXmlToObjectModel.MergeRunContent(DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Wordprocessing.Run,DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Wordprocessing.Run)
member.summary: Moves all content from sourceRun into targetRun, merging adjacent Text elements to reconstruct continuous text strings.

member.name: M:IronWord.APIWrapper.Parse.WordXmlToObjectModel.MergeAdjacentTextElements(DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Wordprocessing.Run)
member.summary:
Merges consecutive DOM-adjacent Text elements within a run into a single Text element. For example, three Text elements "date of {", "expiryDate", "}" become a single Text element "date of {expiryDate}".
Only merges Text elements that are true DOM siblings with no intervening elements (e.g. a Break between two Text elements prevents merging across the break).

member.name: M:IronWord.APIWrapper.Parse.WordXmlToObjectModel.AreRunPropertiesEqual(DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Wordprocessing.Run,DocumentFormat.OpenXml.Wordprocessing.Run)
member.summary: Compares the RunProperties of two runs for equality. Returns true if both runs have identical formatting (or both lack formatting), indicating they were only split by non-content elements like proofErr.

member.name: T:IronWord.License
member.summary: Allows IronWord license keys to be applied globally across an application.

member.name: P:IronWord.License.IsLicensed
member.summary:
Determines whether this instance of IronWord is Licensed.
Will return false unless a is set to a valid trial or full license key in property, Web.Config, App.Config or appsettings.json in .Net Core.
See https://ironsoftware.com/csharp/word/licensing/ and https://ironsoftware.com/csharp/word/docs/license/license-keys/
member.returns: true if the license key given is valid.

member.name: P:IronWord.License.LicenseKey
member.summary:
Get Licensed at https://ironsoftware.com/csharp/word/licensing/
For .Net framework applications, a license key can alternatively be added to Web.Config or App.Config XML file using <add key="IronWord.LicenseKey" value="IRONWORD-LICENSE-KEY"/> within the appSettings node. See https://ironsoftware.com/csharp/word/docs/license/license-keys/
For .Net Core applications, a license key may be added to appsettings.json where the key name is "IronWord.LicenseKey" and the value is a valid IronWord trial or full license key.
See https://ironsoftware.com/csharp/word/licensing/ for licensing options.

member.name: M:IronWord.License.IsValidLicense(System.String)

summary.para: Determines whether a string license is valid upon the validation process

member.param: IronWord license key as a string
member.returns: true if the license key given is valid and not expired.

remarks.para: ❗ Validation Behavior: This method will return false for expired keys, even if they were previously valid. This includes:

para.description: 🗝️ Get a Trial Key: Learn more and get a trial key
🗝️ Commercial Licensing: View commercial licensing options

member.name: M:IronWord.License.DisableAppAnalytics
member.summary: Disables AppAnalytics for IronWord. Limitations apply.

member.summary: Throw exception if license is not set.

member.summary: Gets or sets the document default table style.

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.Container.#ctor
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the Container class.

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.Container.#ctor(IronWord.Models.Abstract.ContentElement[])
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the Container class with the specified child elements.
member.param: The child elements to be added.

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.Container.AddChild(IronSoftware.Abstractions.Word.IWordDocumentObject[])
member.summary: Adds one or more child content elements to the current content element.
member.param: The content elements to be added as children.
member.remarks: Paragraphs, runs, images, shapes, and text will automatically be added to the last section in the document.

member.name: T:IronWord.Models.ContentRange
member.summary: Represents a range of elements within the document content model.

member.name: P:IronWord.Models.ContentRange.IsSinglePoint
member.summary: Gets or sets a value indicating whether the range consists of a single point.

member.name: P:IronWord.Models.ContentRange.StartPosition
member.summary: Gets the starting position of the range within the document.

member.name: P:IronWord.Models.ContentRange.EndPosition
member.summary: Gets the ending position of the range within the document.

member.name: P:IronWord.Models.ContentRange.CoveredElements
member.summary: Gets a read-only array of the content elements covered by the range.

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.ContentRange.#ctor(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Initializes a new instance of the ContentRange class with the specified start and end positions.
member.param: The starting position of the range.
The ending position of the range.
Optional flag indicating whether the range represents a single point.

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.ContentRange.ValidateRange(System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Validates whether the specified start and end positions represent a valid range.
member.param: The starting position to validate.
The ending position to validate.
member.returns: True if the range is valid, false otherwise.

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.ContentRange.SetStartPosition(System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the starting position of the range.
member.param: The new starting position.
member.returns: A new ContentRange object with the updated starting position.

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.ContentRange.SetEndPosition(System.Int32)
member.summary: Sets the ending position of the range.
member.param: The new ending position.
member.returns: A new ContentRange object with the updated ending position.

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.ContentRange.Intersects(IronWord.Models.ContentRange)
member.summary: Checks whether this ContentRange intersects with another ContentRange.
member.param: The other ContentRange to compare with.
member.returns: True if the ranges intersect, false otherwise.

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.ContentRange.GetCoveredElements
member.summary: Gets an array of ContentElements covered by the range.
member.returns: An array of ContentElements.

member.summary: No border specified.

member.summary: No actual border drawn, but space is reserved for a border.

member.summary: A single, solid line border.

member.summary: A thicker, solid line border.

member.summary: A line border with small dots along its length.

member.summary: A line border with a dot, dot, dash pattern.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.BorderValues.Triple

member.summary: A line border with alternating thin, thick, and thin sections, with a small gap between each section.

member.summary: A line border with alternating thin and thick sections, with a medium gap between each section.

member.summary: A line border with alternating thick and thin sections, with a medium gap between each section.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.BorderValues.ThinThickThinMediumGap
member.summary: A line border with alternating thin, thick, and thin sections, with a medium gap between each section.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.BorderValues.ThinThickLargeGap
member.summary: A line border with alternating thin and thick sections, with a large gap between each section.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.BorderValues.ThickThinLargeGap
member.summary: A line border with alternating thick and thin sections, with a large gap between each section.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.BorderValues.ThinThickThinLargeGap
member.summary: A line border with alternating thin, thick, and thin sections, with a large gap between each section.

member.name: T:IronWord.Models.Enums.TextWrap
member.summary: Specifies how text should wrap around a shape in the document.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.TextWrap.None
member.summary: Text does not wrap around the shape and flows without interruption.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.TextWrap.Square
member.summary: Text wraps around the shape in a square fashion, maintaining a constant margin around the shape.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.TextWrap.Tight
member.summary: Text wraps tightly around the shape, conforming to its contours and minimizing the surrounding space.

member.name: T:IronWord.Models.Enums.TextWrapStyle
member.summary: Specifies how text should wrap around an image in the document.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.TextWrapStyle.Inline
member.summary: Text and image appear inline, with text flowing beside the image.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.TextWrapStyle.Square
member.summary: Text wraps around the image in a square fashion, maintaining a constant margin around the image.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.TextWrapStyle.Tight
member.summary: Text wraps tightly around the image, conforming to its contours and minimizing the surrounding space.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.TextWrapStyle.Behind
member.summary: Text appears behind the image and is obscured by it.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.TextWrapStyle.InFront
member.summary: Text appears in front of the image and may overlap it.

member.name: T:IronWord.Models.Enums.VerticalPositionValues
member.summary: Specifies the vertical position of text relative to the baseline.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.VerticalPositionValues.Baseline
member.summary: Text is aligned with the baseline.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.VerticalPositionValues.Subscript
member.summary: Text is displayed as a subscript, positioned below the baseline.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.VerticalPositionValues.Superscript
member.summary: Text is displayed as a superscript, positioned above the baseline.

member.name: T:IronWord.Models.Enums.WrapText
member.summary: Specifies the manner in which an image wraps around text in the document.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.WrapText.None
member.summary: No wrapping occurs. The image sits alongside the text without interfering with its flow.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.WrapText.InLineWithText
member.summary: The image is placed inline with the text, flowing alongside it on the same line.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.WrapText.Square
member.summary: The image is placed within a square frame and positioned alongside the text.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.WrapText.Tight
member.summary: The image is wrapped tightly around the text, conforming to its shape.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.WrapText.Through
member.summary: The text flows through the image, allowing gaps for the image content.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.WrapText.TopAndBottom
member.summary: The image is placed above and below the text, separated by spacing.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.WrapText.BehindText
member.summary: The image is positioned behind the text, appearing only through gaps in the text.

member.name: F:IronWord.Models.Enums.WrapText.InFrontOfText
member.summary: The image is positioned in front of the text, obscuring any text underneath.

member.summary: Converts a Color object to its hexadecimal representation.

member.name: T:IronWord.Models.Color
member.summary:
A universally compatible Color for .NET 7, .NET 6, .NET 5, and .NET Core. As well as compatibility with Windows, NanoServer, IIS, macOS, Mobile, Xamarin, iOS, Android, Google Compute, Azure, AWS, and Linux.
Works nicely with popular Image Color such as , , , .
Implicit casting means that using this class to input and output Color from public APIs gives full compatibility to all Color-types fully supported by Microsoft.

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.Color.FromArgb(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a structure from the specified 8-bit color values (red, green, and blue). The alpha value is implicitly 255 (fully opaque). Although this method allows a 32-bit value to be passed for each color component, the value of each component is limited to 8 bits.
member.param: The red component value for the new . Valid values are 0 through 255.
The green component value for the new . Valid values are 0 through 255.
The blue component value for the new . Valid values are 0 through 255.

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.Color.FromArgb(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32,System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a structure from the specified 8-bit color values (alpha, red, green, and blue). Although this method allows a 32-bit value to be passed for each color component, the value of each component is limited to 8 bits.
member.param: The alpha value for the new . Valid values are 0 through 255.
The red component value for the new . Valid values are 0 through 255.
The green component value for the new . Valid values are 0 through 255.
The blue component value for the new . Valid values are 0 through 255.

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.Color.FromArgb(System.Int32,IronWord.Models.Color)
member.summary:
Creates a structure from the specified structure, but with the new specified alpha value.
Although this method allows a 32-bit value to be passed for the alpha value, the value is limited to 8 bits.
member.param: The alpha value for the new . Valid values are 0 through 255.
The from which to create the new .

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.Color.FromArgb(System.Int32)
member.summary: Creates a structure from a 32-bit ARGB value.
member.param: A value specifying the 32-bit ARGB value.

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.Color.ToString
member.summary: Returns the color as a string in the format: #AARRGGBB.

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.Color.GetLuminance
member.summary: Luminance is a value from 0 (black) to 100 (white) where 50 is the perceptual "middle grey". Luminance = 50 is the equivalent of Y = 18.4, or in other words a 18% grey card, representing the middle of a photographic exposure.
member.returns: Preceived Lightness

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.Color.GetBrightness
member.summary: Calculates the brightness of a color.
member.returns: The brightness of the color, a value between 0 (black) and 1 (white).

member.name: M:IronWord.Models.Color.ToArgb
member.summary:
Gets the 32-bit ARGB value of this structure.
Further Documentation:
Code Example
member.returns: The 32-bit ARGB value of this .
